skill.name: conference-abstract-adaptor
skill.description: Adapt abstracts to meet specific conference word limits and formats.
skill.summary: Reformat and compress academic abstracts for multi-conference submission with automatic word count compliance.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 52 patterns across 4 files; manual review confirms all high-severity findings are false positives. Markdown backticks were misidentified as shell execution, documentation URLs as network access, and benign substrings ('MD', '../') as cryptographic and traversal threats. The Python script performs only local text processing with no network access, no dynamic code execution, and no credential handling. Minor concern: CLI arguments accept arbitrary file paths without validation, which the skill's own security checklist acknowledges.

low_findings.title: Unvalidated CLI File Path Arguments
low_findings.description: The --abstract and --output parameters accept arbitrary file paths from user input without restriction. A user or calling agent could read files outside the workspace (line 119) or overwrite arbitrary files (line 140) by passing paths with traversal sequences. The skill's own security checklist at SKILL.md lines 143-146 documents this risk and recommends validation, but the Python script does not implement any path restrictions.

content.user_title: Adapt Conference Abstracts to Meet Format Requirements
content.value_statement: Researchers often need to submit the same abstract to multiple conferences, each with different word limits and format requirements. This skill automatically adapts your abstract text to meet specific conference guidelines, saving hours of manual reformatting work.
actual_capabilities: Adapt abstract text to match conference-specific word limits
Restructure abstracts into required section formats including Background, Methods, Results, and Conclusion
Support multiple conference formats: ASGCT, ASCO, SfN, AACR, and ASM
Verify word counts and flag abstracts that exceed limits
Save adapted output to specified file paths
limitations: Only supports five pre-configured conference formats
Text compression uses simple truncation rather than intelligent summarization
Cannot verify scientific accuracy of adapted content
Requires abstracts to be provided as text files

use_cases.title: Multi-Conference Submissions
use_cases.description: A researcher needs to submit the same abstract to three conferences with different word limits. The skill reformats the abstract for each target conference automatically.

use_cases.title: Last-Minute Format Compliance
use_cases.description: A graduate student discovers their abstract exceeds the word limit hours before the deadline. The skill compresses the text to meet requirements.

use_cases.title: Conference Format Standardization
use_cases.description: A lab manager needs all team abstracts to follow a consistent structured format. The skill standardizes section headers and formatting across multiple abstracts.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Abstract Adaptation
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt my abstract file at 'abstract.txt' for the ASCO conference. Show me the adapted version and tell me if it meets the word limit.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user submitting to a single conference

prompt_templates.title: Structured Format Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to submit my abstract to ASGCT. Read 'my_abstract.txt', restructure it with Background, Methods, Results, and Conclusion sections, and ensure it stays under 250 words.
prompt_templates.scenario: Restructuring an unstructured abstract for a conference requiring sections

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Conference Batch
prompt_templates.prompt: Take my abstract from 'research_abstract.txt' and prepare versions for ASCO at 260 words, SfN at 2000 characters, and AACR at 300 words. For each, report the original versus adapted word count and flag any that needed compression.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing one abstract for multiple conference submissions

prompt_templates.title: Full Conference Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my abstract file at 'submission.txt' against all five supported conference formats. For each format, show the adapted abstract, confirm section structure compliance, report word count status, and identify which sections lost the most content during compression.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive review before submitting to multiple venues

output_examples.input: Adapt my 350-word neuroscience abstract for SfN submission.
output_examples.output: Your abstract has been adapted for SfN with a 2000-character limit. The original 350 words exceeded the character budget. The adapted version is now 1,980 characters. All content fits within the single-paragraph format required by SfN. Review the compressed sections to verify scientific accuracy before submitting.

output_examples.input: My abstract for ASCO is too long. Can you reduce it from 310 words to 260?
output_examples.output: Analysis complete. Your original abstract was 310 words. After compression to 260 words, the structure maintains the required Background, Methods, Results, and Conclusion sections. The Methods section lost approximately 18 words. The Results and Conclusion sections are unchanged. Review the adapted version for accuracy.

best_practices: Always review compressed abstracts to ensure scientific meaning is preserved
Provide abstracts as plain text files with clear section headers for best restructuring results
Run the script with --list-conferences first to confirm your target conference is supported
anti_patterns: Do not submit compressed abstracts without human review of the scientific content
Do not rely on the skill to generate new scientific content or conclusions
Do not use for unsupported conferences without first editing the conference format configuration

faq.question: Which conferences does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports ASGCT (250 words), ASCO (260 words), SfN (2000 characters), AACR (300 words), and ASM (300 words). Run --list-conferences to see the full list.

faq.question: How does the skill compress text that exceeds the word limit?
faq.answer: It uses simple truncation by keeping the first N words and adding an ellipsis. The compression is basic and does not intelligently summarize or paraphrase content.

faq.question: Can I add a new conference format?
faq.answer: Yes, edit the CONFERENCE_FORMATS dictionary in scripts/main.py to add new conferences with custom word limits and section requirements.

faq.question: Does the skill require internet access?
faq.answer: No, the skill runs entirely offline. It only reads your input file and writes the adapted output locally. No external API calls are made.

faq.question: What file format should my abstract be in?
faq.answer: Your abstract should be a plain text file. For best results, label sections with headers like Background, Methods, Results, and Conclusion.

faq.question: Can the skill verify the scientific accuracy of my adapted abstract?
faq.answer: No, this skill only reformats text by word count and structure. You must review the adapted abstract yourself to ensure it accurately represents your research.
